---
title: "Quaternion Coils"
---

Quaternions give S3 a concrete algebraic model for higher-dimensional phase and rotation-like structure. In this project, the first goal is modest: keep the checked algebra and sign-ambiguity facts visible before any larger geometric or robotics story is told.

The important beginner lesson is that a quaternion state can carry more structure than a point on an ordinary circle. The later spin pages use this to explain why two representatives, `q` and `-q`, can describe the same bounded conjugation action.

::: {.warning-box}
The orientation-debugging note is bounded to `q`/`-q` sign ambiguity under conjugation action. It does not claim a full `SO(3)`, robotics, or quotient-topology formalization.
:::

## What To Notice

- Quaternion coordinates form the checked algebra seed for S3.
- Sign ambiguity is a concrete example of hidden representative choice.
- Broader rotation-space claims remain future work unless theorem cards say otherwise.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

Why should a reader distinguish a checked quaternion algebra fact from a full theorem about physical 3D rotations?

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S3Q-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S3S-T0001"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="S3S-0002"></div>

<p data-paper-ref="papers/S3/PAPER_S3_02_QUATERNION_COILS.md">Paper source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/S3/PAPER_S3_02_QUATERNION_COILS.md">S3 Quaternion Coils</a></p>
<p data-paper-ref="papers/S3/PAPER_S3_04_SPIN_DOUBLE_COVER_ROADMAP.md">Spin source: <a href="https://github.com/corbensorenson/circle-calculus/blob/main/papers/S3/PAPER_S3_04_SPIN_DOUBLE_COVER_ROADMAP.md">S3 Spin Double Cover Roadmap</a></p>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
